steps.title: Distribute the 6
steps.content:
Start by distributing 6 into the second group:
$$6(x^{2}+5) = 6x^{2} + 30$$
Now the full expression becomes:
$$(x^{3}+8x^{2}-7x) + 6x^{2} + 30$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group terms by degree:
  • $x^{3}$ term: $x^{3}$ (appears once)
  • $x^{2}$ terms: $8x^{2} + 6x^{2} = 14x^{2}$
  • $x$ term: $-7x$ (unchanged)
  • Constant: \$30$
The simplified expression is:
$$x^{3} + 14x^{2} - 7x + 30$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
